turns.content: why does this even work this way? I'm trying to load a PNG file using the image crate but my code just crashes immediately. It seems like I have to manually handle memory allocation and then pass ownership around, which feels really clunky compared to how Python or C++ libraries seem to make it feel.

That frustration is common when you first switch from high-level languages to Rust because the image crate enforces strict ownership rules. When you call image::open() on a file path, it returns an OwnedImage which owns the raw buffer internally; you cannot just pass this around like a reference without cloning or converting it into something like BoxedImage or RgbImage first. The key insight is that Rust's safety comes from ensuring memory safety at compile time so you never get double-free errors when resizing or saving formats later, but it means you must explicitly choose between mutable and immutable variants depending on whether your pixel manipulation logic changes the original data.
